description: Japan's largest specialty chemicals trading company (founded 1832), operating as a hybrid trading/manufacturing/R&D group across five segments: Electronics & Energy (formulated epoxy resins for semiconductor packaging and AI servers, photolithography materials, display chemicals), Advanced Materials & Processing (modified epoxy resins, 3D printing), Life & Healthcare (pharmaceutical APIs, cosmetic ingredients, food additives, diagnostics via Nagase Viita/Hayashibara, Prinova), Mobility (engineering plastics, EV materials), and Functional Material (paints, inks, urethane raw materials, pigments, dyes). Key manufacturing subsidiaries include Nagase ChemteX (formulated epoxy resins, photoresists), Nagase Viita (enzymes, trehalose, excipients — formerly Hayashibara), and Prinova (global food ingredient distributor). SACHEM high-purity semiconductor chemicals business in Asia (Japan, China, Korea) acquired 2025. Distributes products from 2,000+ global suppliers to 9,000+ business partners across 27 countries. Revenue FY2024: 944.96B JPY; net income 25.5B JPY; ROE 6.4%. FX-sensitive: yen weakness boosts overseas profits (Americas 187B JPY, Greater China 218B JPY). ACE 2.0 medium-term plan targets ROE ≥ 8% and PBR > 1.0x by FY2025.

majorSuppliers: Shin-Etsu Chemical Co. (4063.T, CMP-0024) — silicone materials; Nagase America distributes Shin-Etsu Silicones internationally
Sumitomo Chemical Co. (4005.T, CMP-0046) — specialty chemicals, plastics raw materials
Asahi Kasei Corporation (3407.T) — resins, engineering plastics, nylon; Nagase Plastics is confirmed distributor; Asahi Kasei Pharma transferred diagnostics business to Nagase in July 2025
DIC Corporation (4631.T) — dyes, pigments, resins for paints/inks industries; Nagase distributes DIC products
Toray Industries (3402.T) — engineering plastics (PBT, PPS); confirmed Nagase Plastics supplier
Teijin Ltd. (3401.T) — engineering plastics; confirmed Nagase Plastics supplier
Tosoh Corporation (4042.T) — specialty chemicals; confirmed Nagase Plastics supplier
Mitsubishi Chemical Group (4188.T) — via Mitsubishi Engineering Plastics; confirmed Nagase Plastics supplier
Idemitsu Kosan (5019.T) — petrochemical resins; confirmed Nagase Plastics supplier
Sekisui Chemical (4204.T) — specialty chemical products; confirmed Nagase Plastics supplier
Toyobo Co. Ltd. (3101.T) — engineering plastics, films; joint venture partner (Xenomax Japan for polyimide film)
Kaneka Corporation (4118.T) — specialty chemicals, functional materials; Nagase America distributes Kaneka products
Nippon Shokubai Co. (4114.T) — specialty chemicals; Nagase America distributes Nippon Shokubai products
BASF Japan Ltd. (private) — specialty chemicals, engineering plastics; confirmed Nagase Plastics supplier
Covestro Japan (private) — polycarbonate and engineering plastics; confirmed Nagase Plastics supplier
Celanese Corporation (CE) — engineering plastics (POM, LCP); confirmed Nagase Plastics supplier
Eastman Chemical Company (EMN) — Tritan copolyester, engineering plastics; Nagase distributes globally
Polyplastics Co., Ltd. (private) — engineering plastics (POM, PBT, LCP); confirmed Nagase Plastics supplier
Nouryon (private, formerly Akzo Nobel Specialty Chemicals) — polymer chemistry; Nagase America distributes
Westlake Epoxy (private) — epoxy resins, curing agents; Nagase America signed distribution agreement Feb 2025

SACHEM Inc. (private, USA) — high-purity semiconductor chemicals (developers, stripping agents, photoresist ancillaries); Nagase acquired SACHEM's Asia business (Japan, China, Korea) for $101M in 2025
majorClients: Semiconductor fab operators — buy photolithography chemicals (developers, stripping agents), encapsulant epoxy resins, wet process chemicals for silicon wafer, DRAM, NAND, logic chip manufacturing

AI server and advanced packaging manufacturers — buy Nagase ChemteX formulated epoxy resins (LMC liquid molding compound) for CoWoS, HBM, and high-end server chip packages; this was the key driver of Electronics & Energy segment growth in FY2024

Display panel makers (LCD, OLED) — buy photolithography materials, siloxane optical materials, chemical management equipment for display processes; Nagase built Inkron Oy (Finland) stake for OLED and touch sensor materials (later sold to Radiant Opto-Electronics for 1.035B JPY)
Murata Manufacturing Co. (6981.T, CMP-0113) — buys electronic materials (epoxy compounds, specialty chemicals) for passive component manufacturing
Toyota Motor Corporation (7203.T) and automotive OEMs — buy engineering plastics, coatings chemicals, battery encapsulation materials via Mobility Solutions department

Paint and coatings manufacturers (Nippon Paint 4612.T, Kansai Paint 4613.T) — buy resin raw materials, pigments, dyes, solvents, urethane raw materials through Nagase Chemical Co. and Performance Chemicals department

Pharmaceutical companies (Takeda 4502.T, Daiichi Sankyo 4568.T, Astellas 4503.T) — buy APIs, pharmaceutical excipients, intermediates through Life & Healthcare Products department; Nagase Viita supplies TREHA trehalose and enzymatic excipients

Cosmetics manufacturers (Shiseido 4911.T, Kao 4452.T, Kosé 4922.T) — buy cosmetic active ingredients including AA2G (stabilized vitamin C derivative), UV filters, surfactants, emollients through Life & Healthcare department

Food and beverage manufacturers (Ajinomoto 2802.T, Asahi Group 2502.T, Kirin 2503.T) — buy food additives, functional ingredients including TREHA trehalose, SUNMALT maltose, amino acids, vitamins through Prinova and Nagase Food Ingredients
Fiber/textile dyeing companies — buy dyes, pigments, auxiliaries through Nagase Chemical Co. and Nishinihon Nagase; core historical business since 1832
Automotive electronics makers (DENSO 6902.T, Murata 6981.T) — buy epoxy encapsulants and specialty materials for in-vehicle electronics
Ink and printing companies — buy resins, pigments, dyes through Nagase Chemical and performance chemicals department
keySubsidiaries: Nagase ChemteX Corporation — develops and manufactures formulated epoxy resins (DENACOL, DENATITE), photolithography materials (photoresists, developers, strippers), semiconductor LMC encapsulants. JV origin: Nagase-CIBA Ltd. 1970 with Ciba-Geigy for epoxy resin manufacturing

Nagase Viita Co., Ltd. — formerly Hayashibara Co. (acquired 2012, renamed 2024); manufactures trehalose (TREHA), maltose (SUNMALT), phospholipids, enzymes, AA2G vitamin C derivative. Net sales FY2025: 35B JPY, 788 employees
Prinova Group LLC — global distributor of food ingredients (amino acids, vitamins, sweeteners, caffeine); acquired 2019. Major North America/Europe presence; sports nutrition focus
Nagase Plastics Co., Ltd. — distributes engineering plastics from Asahi Kasei, Toray, Mitsubishi, Sumitomo, DIC, BASF Japan, Covestro, Celanese, Eastman and others
Nagase Chemical Co., Ltd. — distributes dyestuffs, industrial chemicals (paints, inks, urethane industries)
Nagase Diagnostics Co., Ltd. — diagnostic reagents, enzymes, life science materials; formed July 2025 from Asahi Kasei Pharma's diagnostics business transfer
Nagase Filter Co., Ltd. — manufactures DENAFILTER stainless steel filtration systems for film manufacturing
Nagase RooTAC Industries, Inc. — plastic product manufacturing
Xenomax Japan Co., Ltd. — joint venture with Toyobo (3101.T); manufactures XENOMAX polyimide film (ultra-stable substrate for flexible/micro displays, aerospace solar panels). Established 2018
Nagase Techno-Engineering Co., Ltd. — vacuum equipment, chemical supply systems for semiconductor/LCD manufacturing
keyProducts: DENACOL (epoxy resins) — Nagase ChemteX; specialty epoxy compounds for adhesives, coatings, electronics
DENATITE (formulated epoxy) — Nagase ChemteX; semiconductor encapsulants and display bonding agents
LMC (Liquid Molding Compound) — Nagase ChemteX; semiconductor wafer-level package encapsulants for AI servers and advanced packaging
Photoresists and photolithography chemicals — Nagase ChemteX; positive/negative tone resists for semiconductor and display manufacturing
Resist stripping agents — Nagase ChemteX; for semiconductor fab cleaning processes
TREHA (trehalose) — Nagase Viita; food, pharmaceutical, and cosmetic ingredient
AA2G (ascorbic acid 2-glucoside) — Nagase Viita; stabilized vitamin C derivative for cosmetics
XENOMAX (polyimide film) — Xenomax Japan JV with Toyobo; ultra-stable flexible substrate
SACHEM Envure System — high-purity semiconductor wet chemicals (acquired 2025)
historicalKeyDistributionDeals: Chemical Industry of Basel / Ciba-Geigy (pre-WWII) — chemical products; led to epoxy JV Nagase-CIBA 1970
General Electric (1968) — exclusive Japan distributor; led to GE Engineering Plastics JV 1971
Laporte PLC (2000) — pharmaceutical and agrochemical raw materials
Eastman Chemical (ongoing) — Tritan copolyester and specialty chemicals globally
competitorContext: Brenntag SE (BNR) — German global chemical distributor; #1 globally
IMCD N.V. (IMCD.AS) — Dutch specialty chemical distributor
Mitsubishi Chemical Group (4188.T) — competes in specialty chemicals
Mitsui Chemicals (4183.T) — Japanese chemical manufacturer and distributor
recentCorporateActions: Acquired SACHEM Asia semiconductor chemicals business (Japan, China, Korea) for $101M (March 2025); SACHEM Japan GK and SN Tech became subsidiaries
Acquired Asahi Kasei Pharma diagnostics business; began operations as Nagase Diagnostics Co., Ltd. (July 2025)
Share buyback: 7.5M shares repurchased, 5M shares cancelled under ACE 2.0 plan
Inkron Oy (Finland optical materials for OLED/touch sensors) sold to Radiant Opto-Electronics (Taiwan) for 1.035B JPY (June 2025)
Hayashibara Co. renamed to Nagase Viita Co., Ltd. (April 2024)
ACE 2.0 medium-term plan (FY2021-FY2025): targets ROE ≥ 8%, PBR > 1.0x, total payout ratio 100% for 2 years

FY2024_ended_Mar2025.note: Revenue exceeded 1 trillion JPY on consolidated basis including subsidiaries. Electronics & Energy segment grew on AI server epoxy demand. Life & Healthcare gained from Asahi Kasei diagnostics acquisition.

shareholderStructure.note: Vanguard Group holds 3.7% (major foreign institution). Foreign ownership estimated ~18-22% based on available data; exact figure not publicly confirmed. Prior entry of 47% was likely incorrect.

forexSensitivity.note: Trading company with global operations in 27 countries; revenues in USD, EUR, CNY, and other currencies. Yen weakness boosts gross profit from overseas operations. FY2023: forex impact +5B JPY gross profit, +0.9B JPY operating income. Americas (187B JPY revenue) and Greater China (218B JPY) are significant non-JPY revenue centers. Benefits from weak yen environment.

_metadata.dataSource: Nagase IR (nagase.co.jp), Nagase Annual Report 2024, StockAnalysis, MarketScreener, SharedResearch, Nagase Plastics supplier page, NSM-NA supplier page, SACHEM press releases, Asahi Kasei press releases, Simply Wall St, MarketReportAnalytics
_metadata.notes: Note: User referenced ticker 8102.T but correct ticker is 8012.T. Nagase is Japan's largest specialty chemicals distributor (Asia #1, global #5) with 190+ year history. The company is a hybrid trading/manufacturing/R&D house — not just a pure distributor. Key growth drivers: AI server epoxy resins (Nagase ChemteX LMC products), SACHEM semiconductor chemicals acquisition, Asahi Kasei diagnostics acquisition. PBR calculated as 4701/3679.09 = 1.28x. EPS 230.39 JPY from FY2024 annual financial highlights. Market cap ~480B JPY as of Feb 2026. The correct foreign ownership figure could not be precisely confirmed; 47% in old entry appears too high for this type of mid-cap Japanese trading company.
